overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. LinkedIn demonstrates the strongest individual following, while Bluesky exhibits high engagement in terms of shares.

contentThemes.description: Sharing updates on research projects, publications, and grant awards.
Posting about open positions within the research team.
Announcing upcoming conference presentations and participation.
Sharing updates on software tools for social media analysis.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to improve engagement.
Cross-promote content across platforms to reach a wider audience.
Use relevant hashtags on all platforms to increase discoverability.
Engage with followers in the comments and replies to foster a stronger community.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows good engagement through reposts, indicating a receptive audience for sharing research findings. Leveraging this platform for disseminating paper publications can be highly effective.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is relatively lower compared to other platforms. Content should be tailored to the Mastodon community and involve more community-focused discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is the strongest platform with a large follower base and high engagement with research grant announcements and hiring posts. Maintain a professional tone and focus on career-related content.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
